response: you can use the runtime to get an estimate of memory usage, though exact figures are hard without a profiler. c# memory management follows a garbage collection policy that reclaims memory automatically, so manual clearing is often unnecessary unless you hold references. a simple script can help track object sizes by using the getobjectdata method in some frameworks, but remember that this is just an approximation. even in western software development practices, relying solely on code to measure memory is less reliable than using specialized tools. if you suspect memory leaks, focus on finding lingering references rather than just measuring instance size.
